chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P. No.S-1571 of 2016 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For orders on office objection 2. For katcha peshi. 07.12.2016. Ms. Shaista Shah, Advocate for petitioners alongwith petitioners No.2 and 3. Mr. Shahid Hussain Baloch, Advocate for respondents No.7, 10 and 14. Mr. Ashfaque Nabi Kazi, Assistant A.G a/w SIP Muhammad Uris SHO P.S Kandiaro District Naushahro Feroze. = Today, Ms. Shaista Shah, Advocate is present alongwith petitioners No.2 and 3-Rashid Mangriyo and Gulan Mangriyo and states that petitioners have started residing in their ancestral village in view of the earlier order dated 30.11.2016 but they are under constant threat from private respondents that after disposal of this petition they would cause them harm and injury. In these circumstances, SIP Muhammad Uris SHO of P.S Kandiaro is present, who is directed to call private respondents in Police Station and obtain bond of good behaviour from them. The SHO is further directed to ensure that petitioners are not harassed or coerced to leave their ancestral village in future also. All the parties including police officials/ respondents are forewarned that any violation of Court order passed today or earlier will be construed as contumacious disobedience of the Court orders and the adverse consequences shall follow. In view of the above observations and directions, instant constitutional petition stands disposed of. Copy of this order be communicated to the office of learned A.A.G Sindh as well as SSP Naushahro Feroze by all modes of communication including fax and email for taking necessary action and implementing the order in letter and spirit.

In view of the above observations and directions, instant constitutional petition stands disposed of. Copy of this order be communicated to the office of learned A.A.G Sindh as well as SSP Naushahro Feroze by all modes of communication including fax and email for taking necessary action and implementing the order in letter and spirit. JUDGE Shahid
